what_it_does: Records metadata for a single channel attachment: an image asset posted to the design-assets channel, linking to an image file and recording when it was added.
observations: The file consists only of YAML frontmatter with no body content.
The attachment belongs to the 'design-assets' channel.
The 'added' field is an ISO 8601 timestamp with timezone.
standouts: The target URI contains spaces and parentheses ('image (12)1'), which is unusual for a stable asset pointer.
It is a minimal, single-purpose metadata record.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter for structured metadata.
Fields include id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added.
Target uses a custom 'image://' URI scheme.
concerns: The target path contains spaces and parentheses that may require escaping or encoding in consumers.
There is no checksum, MIME type, dimensions, or file size to verify the asset.
There is no 'added_by' or title/alt text for the attachment.
notes: A minimal ledger entry that registers an image attachment in the design-assets channel with a timestamped target pointer.
questions: What actual file or resource does 'image (12)1' name?
Is the 'image://' URI resolvable and stable?
What is the image format, size, or resolution?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose (a channel attachment metadata record) matches the content: it records an image attachment, its channel, target, and addition time.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to create a lightweight, machine-readable record that ties a channel to an attached image asset and a point-in-time addition event.
contribution: Provides a concrete, timestamped pointer from a channel to a specific image asset, which a fused system would keep as a provenance record for design assets.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-frontmatter attachment ledger entry for an image in the design-assets channel, pointing to image://design-assets/icons/image (12)1 and recording its addition timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To register when a specific image asset was attached to the design-assets channel.
